feed.title: Security Advisory for .NET packages hosted at the NuGet Gallery
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for .NET packages hosted at the NuGet Gallery on GitHub

entry.title: [Magick.NET-Q8-x86] ImageMagick: Invalid MSL <map> can result in a use after free
entry.content:
The MSL interpreter crashes when processing a invalid <map> element that causes it to use an image after it has been freed.

References

  • https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/security/advisories/GHSA-w8mw-frc6-r7m8
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-26983
  • https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/commit/7cfae4da24a995fb05386d77364ff404a7cca7bc
  • https://github.com/dlemstra/Magick.NET/releases/tag/14.10.3
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w8mw-frc6-r7m8

entry.title: [Magick.NET-Q8-x64] ImageMagick: Invalid MSL <map> can result in a use after free
entry.content:
The MSL interpreter crashes when processing a invalid <map> element that causes it to use an image after it has been freed.

  • https://github.com/dlemstra/Magick.NET/releases/tag/14.10.3
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w8mw-frc6-r7m8

entry.title: [Magick.NET-Q8-x86] ImageMagick has a possible infinite loop in its JPEG encoder when using `jpeg:extent`
entry.content:
A continue statement in the JPEG extent binary search loop in the jpeg encoder causes an infinite loop when writing persistently fails. An attacker can trigger a 100% CPU consumption and process hang (Denial of Service) with a crafted image.

References

  • https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/security/advisories/GHSA-gwr3-x37h-h84v
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-26283
  • https://github.com/ImageMagick/ImageMagick/commit/c448c6920a985872072fc7be6034f678c087de9b
  • https://github.com/dlemstra/Magick.NET/releases/tag/14.10.3
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-gwr3-x37h-h84v
